events.context: Monster session 2026-04-02/03: 55 commits. 3 multi-AI brainstorms (Codex+Gemini+Claude). Built: TSL infrastructure (6 files), GLSL effects (caustics, dispersion, Moebius), multi-client MCP control, remote QA pipeline (screenshot+video+metrics), IXQL RENDER/NAVIGATE/QA commands, cinematic planet realism (limb darkening, atmospheric scattering), Saturn ring shader, multilingual planet names.

Float32 catastrophic cancellation pattern: large parent world coords + tiny child offsets = jitter. Fix: camera-parent the orrery. Codex+Gemini identified via multi-AI brainstorm. This pattern applies to ANY Three.js scene with camera-following objects at small scale.
Observe-before-fix pattern: 5 shader changes wasted because root cause was positional, not shader-level. Frame-diff + position-tracking methodology found the real cause. Promoted to feedback memory.
TSL/NodeMaterial incompatibility: Three.js r180 NodeMaterial only works with WebGPURenderer, NOT WebGLRenderer from 3d-force-graph. 6 TSL files built but dormant until renderer migration.

7 named governance visualization concepts: Jurisdictional Pressure (Voronoi), Governance Turbulence (RD), Confidence Topology (circle packing), Governance Viscosity (soft-body), Governance Contagion (CA), Legitimacy Weather (volumetric), Seldon Crisis Patterns (RD parameter shift). + 5 Heckel concepts: Audit Topology (Moebius), The Eclipse (volumetric shadow), Hexavalent Dispersion (6-channel IOR), Constitutional Caustics, Governance Spectroscopy.

effectiveness.proposition: Visual governance representation is compounding — each brainstorm produces named concepts that map techniques to governance meaning. The multi-AI approach consistently outperforms solo analysis for creative-technical synthesis.
